hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 33%, Arizona is getting priced as a middle-tier threat, better than a typical 1-bid team but well below the blue bloods and proven contenders who command 50%+ odds. The sharp money hasn't moved in yet despite Arizona's talent influx and Tommy Lloyd's track record, which means either the market is sleeping on their ceiling or correctly penalizing inconsistency in the Pac-12's unpredictable ecosystem.

trade.question: When will DHS be funded again?: Will legislation that, upon becoming law, results in the Department of Homeland Security being funded at 12:01 AM ET the calendar day after enactment become law before Apr 15, 2026?

daily_take.headline: Arizona's Tournament Odds Jump on What Actual Catalyst?
daily_take.deck: Arizona popped 2.2 points to 33% overnight on $1.7M volume, but the timing is weird. No recent tournament seeding intel, no roster moves, no coaching news that we can pin down. The market's either frontrunning something real or overweighting one good game. Smart bettors should dig into whether this is informed money (draft picks tightening, transfer portal clarity) or just noise chasing a mid-major hot streak. At 33%, Arizona's still fairly priced if they're a 7-8 seed, but overpriced if they're looking at a 10-11.

sidebar.headline: Rubio's nomination odds keep climbing, but GOP primary math suggests this is overstating his lock-in status against better-organized rivals.

sidebar.headline: McIlroy at 6.5% is laughably cheap if he stays healthy through spring, but the injury risk is real and heavily underpriced here.
sidebar.label: Rory McIlroy: 6.5%

sidebar.headline: SpaceX IPO at 20% is a liquidity trap with minimal volume and zero hard signals, treat as noise until something concrete drops.
sidebar.label: SpaceX IPO: 20.0%
